subject: Cloud Computing
title: The $72,000 Mistake
description: A micro-lesson that makes The $72,000 Mistake usable.

hook.text: A developer left a GPU instance running over a holiday weekend. The bill: $72,000 for three days.

buildup.text: GPU instances cost $3-$30 per hour. Forget to shut one down and the meter runs — 24 hours a day.

discovery.text: Billing alerts and budgets can warn you before costs spiral. Most providers let you set spending thresholds.

twist.text: Cloud providers won't stop your spending automatically. Alerts only warn — they don't turn anything off.

climax.text: Companies now build automated kill switches: if spending exceeds a limit, resources shut down automatically.

punchline.text: The cloud never stops charging. Even on holidays.

quiz.question: What is a critical limitation of cloud billing alerts?
options: They warn about spending but don't automatically stop resources from running
They only work for storage costs, not compute
They take two weeks to notify you of overspending
